subject: Science
chapter: Nature of Matter: Elements, Compounds, and Mixtures

key_points: Matter is categorized as mixtures or pure substances based on composition
Mixtures can be separated using physical methods because their components retain their properties
Elements are made of identical atoms and represent the simplest form of matter
Compounds have fixed chemical compositions and new properties different from their elements
Chemical reactions lead to the formation of compounds through rearrangement of atoms
examples: Brass is an alloy made from copper and zinc
Iron sulfide is a compound formed by heating iron with sulfur
Carbon dioxide is a compound formed from carbon and oxygen
Salt solution is a homogeneous mixture
Sand mixed with water forms a heterogeneous mixture
formulas_or_processes: Limewater test: CO₂ + Ca(OH)₂ → CaCO₃ + H₂O
Crystallization: Dissolved substance → solid crystals after cooling
exam_tips: Understand how mixtures differ from compounds in terms of composition and properties
Remember that compounds cannot be separated by physical methods
Practice explaining experiments like electrolysis and iron-sulfur reaction
Be able to identify whether a substance is an element, compound, or mixture
